title: Strike Finance - Open App
meta_description: strike finance opens the official perpetuals app for wallet trading, deposits, withdrawals, margin controls, and docs.
hero_lead: strike finance opens the official app for wallet-based perpetuals trading with order-book execution, margin controls, deposits, withdrawals, and API access.
what_is: Strike Finance is an on-chain perpetuals trading platform with a dedicated execution layer and cross-chain settlement, according to its official docs. The app is built for wallet-based trading, deposits, withdrawals, margin modes, order types, and API access.

how.d: Use the official app domain, then choose the live market or tool you need.
Connect a supported crypto wallet and review any wallet prompts before signing.
Use the in-app deposit flow; validators quote, verify, and settle supported deposits on-chain.
Place orders, monitor margin, and use reduce-only, stop-loss, or take-profit controls where available.

points.p: Trading activity runs through the Strike execution layer while settlement and custody are anchored to blockchain contracts.
The deposit and withdrawal docs describe validator-verified transfers across supported chains.
The official app serves active traders, while the documented API supports bots, dashboards, and custom integrations.

faq.a: strike finance is a perpetuals trading app with a dedicated execution layer and on-chain settlement, described in the official documentation.

faq.q: Is strike finance the official app?
faq.a: This page is not the app; the official trading app is hosted at app.strikefinance.org. Always verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

faq.q: What can I do on strike finance?
faq.a: You can access perpetuals markets, manage positions, use margin controls, deposit or withdraw supported assets, and review API resources for programmatic access.

faq.q: Which networks does strike finance use?
faq.a: The docs describe Cardano and Ethereum support for deposits and withdrawals, with settlement handled through on-chain locker contracts and validator verification.
Yes. Perpetuals, leverage, smart contracts, liquidations, and digital asset volatility can all create loss risk; read the official materials before trading.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify app.strikefinance.org before connecting a wallet. Educational only, not financial advice.
